title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("OWNER/reactablePlus")

what: Two ways to build editable tables
title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: reactablePlus offers two abstraction levels. Pick one fits use case.

previous_headings: Two ways to build editable tables
title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: lower-level module accepts plain lists describing rows columns. control every column type (dropdown, numeric, checkbox, toggle, date, text) can wire gating, selection, reset behavior. Use validate_col_spec() validate_row_spec() catch configuration errors app launches.
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  row_spec <- list(   id_col   = "id",   id_vals  = c("row1", "row2", "row3"),   labels   = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"),   selected = rep(TRUE, 3) )  col_spec <- list(   list(col_name = "role",  type = "dropdown",        choices = c("Analyst", "Engineer", "Manager")),   list(col_name = "score", type = "numeric",        min = 0, max = 100, step = 1),   list(col_name = "active", type = "checkbox") )  ui <- fluidPage(   editable_table_ui("demo") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   editable_table_server(     "demo",     data_r     = reactive(NULL),     row_spec   = row_spec,     col_spec   = col_spec   ) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

previous_headings: Two ways to build editable tables
title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: higher-level module uses table_config() widget_col() declaratively describe table renders picker widgets cell. handles mutual exclusion, fill-, gear toggles, data marshaling box.
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = c("PK", "K", "01", "02"),   row_labels = c("PreK", "Kindergarten", "1st Grade", "2nd Grade"),   columns    = list(     widget_col("school", "school_picker", "School", min_width = 300),     widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", width = 200),     widget_col("notes", "notes_input", "Notes", min_width = 150)   ) )  ui <- fluidPage(   config_table_ui("history", cfg) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   config_table_server("history", cfg,     data_r    = reactive(NULL),     search_fn = function(query, grade_key) {       # Return a data frame of matching schools       data.frame(name = "Example School", nces_id = "123456")     }   ) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: don’t use table modules. Every picker widget works standalone Shiny input.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   schoolPickerInput("my_school", grade_key = "PK",                     grade_label = "PreK"),   attendancePickerInput("my_att", grade_label = "PreK"),   notesInput("my_notes", placeholder = "Type here...") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$my_school, {     message("School selected: ", input$my_school$name)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: placing widgets inside reactable::colDef(cell = ...), two things required: Call useReactablePlus() UI load JS/CSS page level (reactable’s React renderer doesn’t process htmlDependency objects). Wrap reactable widget bindPickersOnRender() initialize Shiny input bindings table DOM created.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   reactable::reactableOutput("tbl") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$tbl <- reactable::renderReactable({     tbl <- reactable::reactable(       data.frame(grade = c("PK", "K"), notes = c("", "")),       columns = list(         notes = reactable::colDef(           html = TRUE,           cell = function(value, index) {             as.character(notesInput(               paste0("note_", index),               value = value,               placeholder = "Add a note"             ))           }         )       )     )     bindPickersOnRender(tbl)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Browse Reference full documentation every function. See Config-Driven Table Module docs mutual exclusion, fill-, gear toggle configuration. See Raw-Spec Table Module docs gating, selection, reset behavior.

what: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: Enhanced interactive tables inline editors Shiny.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: reactablePlus extends reactable inline editing widgets declarative configuration layer building editable tables Shiny applications.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: SchoolPicker — typeahead school search popover AttendancePicker — multi-section attendance rating popover HomeschoolPicker — homeschool details popover NotesInput — inline text input Shiny binding GearPopover — settings gear icon toggle switches

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: Shiny module (editable_table_ui / editable_table_server) driven declarative row_spec, col_spec, table_config objects. module handles rendering, input collection, validation, selection gating, reset, data marshaling without domain-specific knowledge.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("OWNER/reactablePlus")

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   reactableOutput("table") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$table <- renderReactable({     tbl <- reactable(my_data, columns = list(       notes = colDef(html = TRUE, cell = function(value, index) {         as.character(notesInput(paste0("notes_", index), value = value))       })     ))     bindPickersOnRender(tbl)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: creation reactablePlus, schoolhistory package becomes thin domain layer Imports: reactablePlus. retains school-history-specific configuration demo apps.

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
code: # BEFORE school_history_config <- function() {   table_config(     columns = list(       col_spec("school", "school_picker", "School", ...),       col_spec("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", ...),       ...     ),     ...   ) }  school_history_ui <- function(id) {   editable_table_ui(id, school_history_config()) }  school_history_server <- function(id, ...) {   editable_table_server(id, school_history_config(), ...) }  # AFTER school_history_config <- function() {   table_config(     columns = list(       widget_col("school", "school_picker", "School", ...),       widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", ...),       ...     ),     ...   ) }  school_history_ui <- function(id) {   config_table_ui(id, school_history_config()) }  school_history_server <- function(id, ...) {   config_table_server(id, school_history_config(), ...) }

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: Replace instances : - library(schoolhistory) → library(reactablePlus) (add ) - useSchoolHistory() → useReactablePlus() - col_spec(...) → widget_col(...) - editable_table_ui(id, config) → config_table_ui(id, config) - editable_table_server(...) → config_table_server(...)

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
code: Imports:     reactablePlus,     shiny,     bslib,     dplyr,     reactable

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: completing renames, run existing shinytest2 suite: tests test-shinytest2.R pass without modification function renames applied, since test end--end behavior rather internal function names.
code: shinytest2::test_app("path/to/schoolhistory/testapp")

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: Creates attendance picker widget renders dashed-border trigger button empty pill summary filled. Clicking opens popover configurable radio groups optional notes textarea.

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
code: attendancePickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   sections = NULL,   trigger_label = "+ Mark attendance",   popover_title = "Attendance",   show_notes = TRUE,   notes_placeholder = "Absences, tardies, context for this school year…",   width = "100%" )

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value returned server named list keyed section's key, plus notes notes enabled, NULL nothing set. value Optional initial value — named list keyed section keys character values. grade_label Optional display label row's grade (e.g. "3rd grade"). Used trigger's aria-label screen reader context. sections list section definitions popover body. section list : key Character. key returned value list (e.g. "school", "class_"). label Character. heading radio group. levels Character vector. radio options. pill_prefix Character (optional). Text prepended pill display section filled (e.g. "Class: " produces "Class: Excellent"). Default prefix — raw level shown. pill_icon Character (optional). Pill icon style: "school" (default first section), "pencil", NULL icon. impact_display List (optional). present, renders sub-line instead pill section. named entries mapping level values display strings, e.g. list(Yes = "\u26A0 impacts academics", = "academic impact"). Defaults school history attendance sections (School Attendance, Class Attendance, Impacts Academics). trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Mark attendance". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Attendance". show_notes Logical. Whether include notes textarea popover. Default TRUE. notes_placeholder Character. Placeholder notes textarea. Default "Absences, tardies, context school year\u2026". width CSS width. Default "100%".

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: htmltools::tagList suitable use Shiny UI, including inside reactable::colDef(cell = ...) renderers.

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: popover's radio sections fully configurable via sections parameter, making widget reusable multi-field rating scenario (behavior tracking, therapy progress, etc.).

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default school-history attendance (backward compatible) attendancePickerInput("att_pk", grade_label = "PreK")  # Pre-filled attendancePickerInput("att_k",   value = list(school = "Good", class_ = "Excellent", impacts = "No"),   grade_label = "Kindergarten" )  # Custom sections for behavior tracking attendancePickerInput("beh_pk",   sections = list(     list(key = "behavior", label = "Classroom Behavior",          levels = c("Exemplary", "Meets Expectations",                     "Needs Improvement", "Unsatisfactory")),     list(key = "participation", label = "Participation",          levels = c("Active", "Moderate", "Minimal"))   ),   trigger_label = "+ Rate behavior",   popover_title = "Behavior",   notes_placeholder = "Context, observations\u2026" ) } # }

what: Collect inline Shiny input values into a tidy tibble — collect_inputs
title: Collect inline Shiny input values into a tidy tibble — collect_inputs
text: Reads inline input Shiny input object using convention paste0(row_id, "_", col_name). input touched user (.e. NULL), value taken fallback_df instead. Gating enforced server-side: condition field's gate list fails given row, field's value forced NA regardless DOM state.

title: Collect inline Shiny input values into a tidy tibble — collect_inputs
code: collect_inputs(   input,   row_ids,   col_spec,   id_col,   fallback_df = NULL,   input_overrides = NULL )

title: Collect inline Shiny input values into a tidy tibble — collect_inputs
text: input Shiny input object moduleServer. row_ids vector row identifier values (typically id_col column display data frame). col_spec validated col_spec list. id_col single character string naming id column. fallback_df tibble merge_existing_data() used fallback input yet set user. input_overrides reactiveValues server-controlled overrides. Checked input[[local_id]] reset deselection wipes take effect reactive flush without browser round-trip.

title: Collect inline Shiny input values into a tidy tibble — collect_inputs
text: tibble one row per element row_ids one column per col_spec entry, plus id column.

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: Server function config-driven editable table module. Pairs config_table_ui(). Uses table_config() objects drive picker widget rendering, state management, cross-column interactions.

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
code: config_table_server(   id,   config,   data_r = shiny::reactive(NULL),   search_fn = NULL )

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: id Module namespace ID. config table_config object. data_r Reactive returning NULL (new/add mode) data frame (edit mode saved rows). search_fn function (query, limit) → data.frame server-side typeahead search. Required column uses school_picker type. Default NULL.

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: list(get_data = reactive).

title: Config-driven Editable Table UI — config_table_ui
text: UI function config-driven editable table module. Works table_config() objects picker widgets, opposed lower-level editable_table_ui() uses raw row_spec/col_spec lists.

title: Config-driven Editable Table UI — config_table_ui
code: config_table_ui(id, config)

title: Config-driven Editable Table UI — config_table_ui
text: id Module namespace ID. config table_config object.

title: Config-driven Editable Table UI — config_table_ui
text: tagList dependencies, toolbar, reactable output.

what: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
title: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
text: Renders reactable table one inline Shiny input per col_spec column per row. row_spec$selectable TRUE, checkbox column prepended clicking anywhere row toggles row's selection.

title: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
code: editable_table_server(   id,   data_r,   row_spec,   col_spec,   existing_data_r = NULL,   reactable_options = list() )

title: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
text: id string. module namespace ID. data_r reactive data frame containing least columns declared row_spec. row_spec validated row_spec list (see validate_row_spec). col_spec validated col_spec list (see validate_col_spec). existing_data_r reactive data frame previously saved values, NULL (default) blank start. reactable_options plain list additional arguments passed reactable::reactable(). Module-controlled arguments (data, columns, onClick) protected overridden. Defaults can overridden include sortable, pagination, highlight, bordered, striped.

title: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
text: named list two reactives: current_data reactive tibble id_col + one column per col_spec entry, updated continuously user interacts. selected_ids reactive vector id_col values checked rows. NULL row_spec$selectable TRUE.

title: Server function for the editable table Shiny module — editable_table_server
text: Reset button wipes inputs clears selections.

what: Check if a grade falls within a school's grade range — gradeInRange

title: Check if a grade falls within a school's grade range — gradeInRange
code: gradeInRange(grade_key, low_grade, high_grade)

title: Check if a grade falls within a school's grade range — gradeInRange
text: grade_key Character. grade check (e.g. "05"). low_grade Character. school's lowest grade (e.g. "K"). high_grade Character. school's highest grade (e.g. "08").

title: Check if a grade falls within a school's grade range — gradeInRange
text: Logical.

title: Check if a grade falls within a school's grade range — gradeInRange
code: gradeInRange("05", "K", "08")   # TRUE #> [1] TRUE gradeInRange("09", "K", "08")   # FALSE #> [1] FALSE gradeInRange("PK", "PK", "12")  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
text: Creates homeschool picker widget. empty, renders dashed-border trigger button. filled, renders pill summary optional provider curriculum details.

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
code: homeschoolPickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   grade_key = NULL,   providers = c("Mother", "Father", "Grandmother", "Grandfather", "Guardian", "Tutor",     "Co-op", "Online program", "Other"),   provider_label = "Who provided homeschooling?",   curriculum_label = "Curriculum / program",   curriculum_placeholder = "e.g. Time4Learning, Sonlight, custom",   show_curriculum = TRUE,   show_notes = TRUE,   notes_placeholder = "Evaluator, co-op participation, any context…",   trigger_label = "+ Mark homeschool",   trigger_sub_label = "(details optional)",   popover_title = "Homeschool details",   popover_title_sub = "(optional)",   filled_pill_label = "Homeschool",   clear_label = "remove homeschool",   ns = "",   width = "100%" )

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value named list , by_other, curriculum, notes active, NULL inactive. value Optional initial value — list combination fields . Pass list() active details. Pass NULL (default) inactive. grade_label Optional display label accessible naming. grade_key grade's two-char key (e.g. "PK", "03"). providers Character vector provider dropdown options. last element "", additional free-text input shown "" selected. Default: c("Mother", "Father", "Grandmother", "Grandfather", "Guardian", "Tutor", "Co-op", "Online program", ""). provider_label Character. Label provider dropdown. Default "provided homeschooling?". curriculum_label Character. Label curriculum text input. Default "Curriculum / program". curriculum_placeholder Character. Placeholder curriculum field. Default "e.g. Time4Learning, Sonlight, custom". show_curriculum Logical. Whether include curriculum field. Default TRUE. show_notes Logical. Whether include notes textarea. Default TRUE. notes_placeholder Character. Placeholder notes textarea. Default "Evaluator, co-op participation, context\u2026". trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Mark homeschool". trigger_sub_label Character NULL. Sub-label trigger button. Default "(details optional)". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Homeschool details". popover_title_sub Character NULL. Popover header sub-text. Default "(optional)". filled_pill_label Character. Text primary pill active. Default "Homeschool". clear_label Character. Text footer clear/remove link. Default "remove homeschool". ns Shiny module namespace prefix. Default "". width CSS width. Default "100%".

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
text: tag attached dependencies.

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
text: Clicking empty trigger immediately flags row active opens details popover. popover fields (provider, curriculum, notes) optional. provider dropdown options, field labels, trigger text configurable, making widget reusable "flag + details" pattern (e.g. therapy provider tracking, tutoring arrangements).

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default school-history homeschool (backward compatible) homeschoolPickerInput("hs_PK", grade_label = "PreK", grade_key = "PK")  # Pre-filled homeschoolPickerInput("hs_K",   value = list(by = "Mother", curriculum = "Time4Learning"),   grade_label = "Kindergarten",   grade_key = "K" )  # Custom use: therapy provider tracking homeschoolPickerInput("therapy_PK",   providers = c("Occupational Therapist", "Speech Therapist",                 "Physical Therapist", "Behavioral Therapist", "Other"),   provider_label = "Type of therapy",   curriculum_label = "Provider / agency",   curriculum_placeholder = "e.g. ABC Therapy Group",   trigger_label = "+ Add therapy",   trigger_sub_label = NULL,   popover_title = "Therapy details",   popover_title_sub = NULL,   filled_pill_label = "Therapy",   clear_label = "remove therapy",   grade_label = "PreK",   grade_key = "PK" ) } # }

what: Generate a stable, unique input element ID — make_input_id
title: Generate a stable, unique input element ID — make_input_id
text: Combines namespace prefix, row identifier, column name single dash-separated string suitable use HTML element ID CSS targeting.

title: Generate a stable, unique input element ID — make_input_id
code: make_input_id(ns_prefix, row_id, col_name)

title: Generate a stable, unique input element ID — make_input_id
text: ns_prefix single non-empty character string — typically Shiny module namespace. row_id scalar (integer character) uniquely identifying row. Must NA. col_name single non-empty character string matching col_name field col_spec entry.

title: Generate a stable, unique input element ID — make_input_id
text: single character string form "<ns_prefix>-<row_id>-<col_name>".

what: Merge previously saved values onto the display data frame — merge_existing_data
title: Merge previously saved values onto the display data frame — merge_existing_data
text: Left-joins existing_data onto data_df using id column declared row_spec. Rows data_df match existing_data receive NA every col_spec column. Rows existing_data absent data_df silently dropped.

title: Merge previously saved values onto the display data frame — merge_existing_data
code: merge_existing_data(data_df, existing_data, row_spec, col_spec)

title: Merge previously saved values onto the display data frame — merge_existing_data
text: data_df tibble containing least id_col display columns declared row_spec. existing_data tibble previously saved values (id_col + col_spec columns), NULL blank start. row_spec validated row_spec list. col_spec validated col_spec list.

title: Merge previously saved values onto the display data frame — merge_existing_data
text: tibble exactly id_col, display_cols, col_spec columns — order. One row per row data_df.

what: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
title: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
text: Extends 'reactable' inline editing widgets (pickers, popovers, text inputs) declarative configuration layer building editable tables Shiny applications. Provides generic editable table module driven row column specifications, plus library cell-level input widgets custom Shiny bindings.

title: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
text: Maintainer: First Last first.last@example.com

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
text: Creates school picker widget typeahead search. empty, renders dashed-border trigger button. filled, renders card showing school name, public/private pill, district, city/state, NCES ID, action links (change, fill , clear).

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
code: schoolPickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   grade_key = NULL,   show_nces_id = TRUE,   trigger_label = "+ Pick school",   popover_title = "Find school",   search_placeholder = "Search by school name, city, district…",   empty_hint = "Type 2+ characters to search 100k+ US schools",   no_match_hint = "No schools match. Check spelling — picking is required.",   show_fill_down = TRUE,   ns = "",   width = "100%" )

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value named list id, name, district, city, state, type, NULL. value Optional initial value — list school fields . grade_label Optional display label (e.g. "3rd grade") accessible naming. grade_key grade's two-char key (e.g. "PK", "03"). Used fill-action tell server grade triggered . show_nces_id Logical. Whether show NCES ID filled state search results. Default TRUE. trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Pick school". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Find school". search_placeholder Character. Placeholder search input. Default "Search school name, city, district\u2026". empty_hint Character. Hint text shown user types. Default "Type 2+ characters search 100k+ US schools". no_match_hint Character. Text shown search returns results. Default "schools match. Check spelling \u2014 picking required.". show_fill_down Logical. Whether show "fill " action link filled state. Default TRUE. ns Character. Shiny module namespace prefix search communication. Pass session$ns("") within module. top-level apps, leave "". width CSS width. Default "100%".

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
text: htmltools::tagList.

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
text: Search server-side: widget sends queries R via input$<ns>school_search; use useSchoolSearch() server function wire response. Trigger popover labels configurable widget can reused server-side typeahead scenario (provider search, facility lookup, etc.).

title: School picker input — schoolPickerInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default (backward compatible) schoolPickerInput("school_PK", grade_label = "PreK", grade_key = "PK")  # Custom labels for provider search schoolPickerInput("provider_PK",   trigger_label = "+ Find provider",   popover_title = "Search providers",   search_placeholder = "Type provider name or specialty\u2026",   empty_hint = "Type 2+ characters to search providers",   no_match_hint = "No providers found.",   show_nces_id = FALSE,   show_fill_down = FALSE,   grade_label = "PreK",   grade_key = "PK" ) } # }

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: Creates declarative configuration object drives config_table_ui() / config_table_server(). config describes rows columns appear, picker widget column uses, cross-column interactions (mutual exclusion, fill-) work.

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
code: table_config(   row_keys,   row_labels,   columns,   gear_toggles = NULL,   interactions = list(),   from_saved_fn = NULL,   to_output_fn = NULL,   toolbar_stats_fn = NULL,   search_fn_col = NULL,   badge_col = "grade",   year_col = "school_year",   year_range = c(1990L, 2050L) )

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: row_keys Character vector row identifiers (e.g. grade keys). row_labels Character vector display labels (length row_keys). columns List column specs built widget_col(). gear_toggles Named list toggle definitions gearPopoverInput(). Pass NULL gear icon. interactions List optional mutual_exclusion fill_down entries. See Details. from_saved_fn Function (db_row, col_specs) -> named list. Converts one row saved data row-state list keyed column ID. Called per row data_r() provides data frame. to_output_fn Function (row_state, row_key) -> single-row data.frame. Converts one row state back output schema. toolbar_stats_fn Function (rows, row_keys) -> shiny::HTML string toolbar. Pass NULL toolbar. search_fn_col Character. Column ID requires server-side search (.e. uses useSchoolSearch()). Pass NULL column needs search. Default NULL. badge_col Character. Column ID rendered static badge row key/label. Default "grade". Pass NULL omit. year_col Character. Column ID editable year spinner. Default "school_year". Pass NULL omit. year_range Integer vector length 2, e.g. c(1990, 2050).

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: table_config list (S3 class "table_config").

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: interactions$mutual_exclusion: list rules, list when_on (column ID), clears (column ID), display (HTML string show cleared column's cell). interactions$fill_down: list column (column ID) range_check (logical — TRUE, uses gradeInRange() limit).

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = c("PK", "K", "01"),   row_labels = c("PreK", "K", "1st"),   columns    = list(     widget_col("school", "school_picker", "School", min_width = 300),     widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", width = 200)   ) ) } # }

what: Include all reactablePlus widget dependencies — useReactablePlus

what: Wire up server-side school search — useSchoolSearch
title: Wire up server-side school search — useSchoolSearch
text: Call Shiny server function (module server). observes shared school_search input sent school pickers routes results back correct widget.

title: Wire up server-side school search — useSchoolSearch
code: useSchoolSearch(input, session, search_fn, limit = 25L)

title: Wire up server-side school search — useSchoolSearch
text: input Shiny input object. session Shiny session object. search_fn function takes (query, limit) returns data frame matching schools. limit Maximum number results return. Default 25.

title: Wire up server-side school search — useSchoolSearch

title: Validate a col_spec list — validate_col_spec
text: Checks col_spec well-formed rendering data manipulation occurs. Throws informative error first problem found.

title: Validate a col_spec list — validate_col_spec
code: validate_col_spec(col_spec, row_spec = NULL)

title: Validate a col_spec list — validate_col_spec
text: col_spec non-empty list column definition lists. entry must contain col_name (string), label (string), type (one "dropdown" "numeric"). Dropdown entries also require choices (list lists, label value, values type). Numeric entries also require min (numeric). row_spec validated row_spec list, NULL. Required gate condition uses type = "selected", requires row_spec$selectable == TRUE.

title: Validate a col_spec list — validate_col_spec
text: Called side-effect. Returns invisibly success.

what: Validate user-supplied input values against col_spec rules — validate_row_inputs
title: Validate user-supplied input values against col_spec rules — validate_row_inputs
text: Checks every row × col_spec-column combination df : Numeric columns: must non-NA, whole number, >= min. Dropdown columns: must non-NA one declared choice values.

title: Validate user-supplied input values against col_spec rules — validate_row_inputs
code: validate_row_inputs(df, row_spec, col_spec)

title: Validate user-supplied input values against col_spec rules — validate_row_inputs
text: df tibble produced collect_inputs(), containing id_col one column per col_spec entry. row_spec validated row_spec list (used identify id column). col_spec validated col_spec list.

title: Validate user-supplied input values against col_spec rules — validate_row_inputs
text: list two fields: valid Logical. TRUE errors found. errors list error entries, list row_id, col_name, message.

title: Validate a row_spec list — validate_row_spec
text: Checks row_spec well-formed rendering data manipulation occurs. Throws informative error first problem found.

title: Validate a row_spec list — validate_row_spec
code: validate_row_spec(row_spec)

title: Validate a row_spec list — validate_row_spec
text: row_spec list fields id_col (string) display_cols (list lists, col_name label).

title: Validate a row_spec list — validate_row_spec
text: Called side-effect. Returns invisibly success.

what: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: Defines single column table_config(). widget column maps picker widget type (school_picker, attendance_picker, homeschool_picker, notes_input, custom) carries widget-specific options.

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
code: widget_col(   id,   type,   label,   width = NULL,   min_width = NULL,   options = list(),   gear_toggle = NULL,   triggers_rerender = FALSE,   empty_value = NULL,   render_cell_fn = NULL,   validate_fn = NULL )

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: id Character. Column ID — becomes key row state prefix Shiny input IDs (e.g. "school" -> input$school_PK). type Character. Widget type: "school_picker", "attendance_picker", "homeschool_picker", "notes_input", "custom". label Character. Column header text. width Integer NULL. Fixed column width pixels. min_width Integer NULL. Minimum column width. options Named list widget-specific options passed widget constructor (e.g. list(show_nces_id = TRUE) school picker, list(sections = ...) attendance picker). gear_toggle Character NULL. gear toggle key controls column's visibility. toggle , column hidden (set show = FALSE). triggers_rerender Logical. TRUE, changes column bump render key (forcing full table re-render). Needed columns involved mutual exclusion. Default FALSE. empty_value default value empty row. Default NULL. render_cell_fn Function (ns, row_key, row_state, col_spec, settings) -> HTML string. needed type = "custom". validate_fn Function (value) -> validated value. Controls raw input cleaned storage. Default NULL uses type-appropriate validation.

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: widget_col list (S3 class "widget_col").
